perturbation.original_text: The Company undertakes to return all such documentation at the request of the Client and, in any event, upon conclusion of the Company's need thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company undertakes to return all such documentation within 30 days of a written request by the Client and may retain a copy for archival purposes indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause required the Company to return documentation upon request or when no longer needed. The modified clause introduces a 30-day timeframe for return but allows the Company to retain copies indefinitely. This contradicts the initial intent of complete confidentiality and creates a conflict if the Client expects all copies to be returned at some point. The introduction of 'archival purposes' without specifying restrictions will create a conflict with the original clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company acknowledges that all Confidential Information made available or disclosed to it by the Client shall remain the exclusive property of the Client and recognises that such documentation is confidential and proprietary to the Client and shall be treated as such and shall not be disclosed to a third party, save as provided in Clause 3 hereunder or as required by any applicable laws, without the prior written consent of the Client. The Company undertakes to return all such documentation at the request of the Client and, in any event, upon conclusion of the Company's need thereof. The Company shall only retain copies of any Confidential Information if necessary in completing the provision of the Services. Any Confidential Information shall at all times be stored securely and the Company shall take reasonable precautions to prevent any unauthorised disclosure. Employee’s and agents of the Company shall only be given access to the Confidential Information in order to proceed with the Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the company to return all documentation upon request or when no longer needed. The changed text allows the Company to retain copies indefinitely for archival purposes. The contradicted text states that all confidential information remains the exclusive property of the client and requires the Company to return all such documentation. The changed text, by allowing indefinite retention of copies, directly contradicts the original intent of complete confidentiality and the requirement to return all documentation, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition, as the obligations are now inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: Employee’s and agents of the Company shall only be given access to the Confidential Information in order to proceed with the Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Employee’s, temporary contractors and agents of the Company may be granted access to Confidential Information to proceed with the Services provided they sign individual confidentiality waivers approved by the Company's legal counsel.
perturbation.explanation: This change expands the scope of individuals who can access Confidential Information to include temporary contractors, contingent upon signing confidentiality waivers. This potentially contradicts the previous limitation to 'employees and agents,' as it introduces a new category with a different level of assurance. The addition of 'temporary contractors' and 'individual confidentiality waivers approved by the Company's legal counsel' introduce ambiguity about enforcement, since there is no clause that enforces it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company acknowledges that all Confidential Information made available or disclosed to it by the Client shall remain the exclusive property of the Client and recognises that such documentation is confidential and proprietary to the Client and shall be treated as such and shall not be disclosed to a third party, save as provided in Clause 3 hereunder or as required by any applicable laws, without the prior written consent of the Client. The Company undertakes to return all such documentation at the request of the Client and, in any event, upon conclusion of the Company's need thereof. The Company shall only retain copies of any Confidential Information if necessary in completing the provision of the Services. Any Confidential Information shall at all times be stored securely and the Company shall take reasonable precautions to prevent any unauthorised disclosure. Employee’s and agents of the Company shall only be given access to the Confidential Information in order to proceed with the Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Employee’s and agents of the Company shall only be given access to the Confidential Information', while the changed text expands this scope to include 'temporary contractors' if they sign waivers. The contradicted text also includes the original restriction of access to employees and agents. The change therefore creates a contradiction by adding a new category of individuals with access to confidential information, potentially weakening the original clause's enforceability and creating uncertainty regarding the access rights of temporary contractors compared to the explicitly named employees and agents.

perturbation.original_text: 3.The Company hereby discloses that it may subcontract all or part of the Services provided to the Client. In such event the Company will use its reasonable endeavours to ensure that all of its Subcontractors, to whom disclosure of the Information is made, act in accordance with the terms of this Agreement as if each were a party hereto. Any third party to whom Confidential Information is provided shall be informed of the confidential nature of the information disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.The Company may subcontract all or part of the Services provided to the Client without restriction. The Company is not responsible for ensuring that its Subcontractors act in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Any third party to whom Confidential Information is provided shall be informed of the confidential nature of the information disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, the Company had an obligation to use reasonable efforts to ensure Subcontractors complied with the agreement. Now, this obligation is removed entirely, and the Company is not responsible. This contradicts the overall purpose of the NDA, as it allows for unmonitored disclosure through subcontractors, therefore this perturbation is in text, since an NDA is supposed to be protected by any means, with this clause going against that.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company acknowledges that all Confidential Information made available or disclosed to it by the Client shall remain the exclusive property of the Client and recognises that such documentation is confidential and proprietary to the Client and shall be treated as such and shall not be disclosed to a third party, save as provided in Clause 3 hereunder or as required by any applicable laws, without the prior written consent of the Client. The Company undertakes to return all such documentation at the request of the Client and, in any event, upon conclusion of the Company's need thereof. The Company shall only retain copies of any Confidential Information if necessary in completing the provision of the Services. Any Confidential Information shall at all times be stored securely and the Company shall take reasonable precautions to prevent any unauthorised disclosure. Employee’s and agents of the Company shall only be given access to the Confidential Information in order to proceed with the Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text placed an obligation on the company to ensure its subcontractors acted in accordance with the agreement. The changed text removes this obligation, stating the company is 'not responsible'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the company to treat confidential information as proprietary, not to disclose it to third parties, and to take reasonable precautions to prevent unauthorized disclosure. By removing the obligation for subcontractors to comply with the agreement, the change allows subcontractors to disclose confidential information without the Company being responsible, which breaks the guarantee of confidentiality in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2The Company acknowledges that damages may not adequately compensate for a breach of this Agreement, and that the Client may also seek such other remedies, including injunctions, as it desires.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2 The Company acknowledges that damages are the sole and adequate compensation for any breach of this Agreement, and the Client waives all rights to seek other remedies, including injunctions.
perturbation.explanation: This completely reverses the remedies available to the Client in case of a breach. Originally, damages were considered inadequate, and the Client could seek injunctions. Now, damages are the sole remedy, and injunctions are waived. This creates a contradiction because the original clause implied broader protection for the Client's confidential information, while the modification limits this protection severely. Making it hard for the client to be protected. This will affect the definition because there is a new condition in the contract, where the confidential information is not protected by anything other than a fine.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this agreement: Confidential Information means any information disclosed (whether actual or potential, whether before or after the date of this Agreement, whether in writing, in electronic format, verbally or by any other means and whether directly or indirectly) by or on behalf of the Client to the Company, which relates to the Services including but not exclusively information about venues, prints, contractors, customers, data, employees, sponsors, suppliers, policies, prices, discounts, business development, financial plans, marketing development, manpower plans, methods, services, ideas, projects, rights, products, project development and all other information which might cause considerable harm to the Client and/or to the Client’s business were it to be available and/or used by any third party; Services means the provision of translation and other language services.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6.2 directly contradicts the implicit protection afforded to the Client's Confidential Information as defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The original Section 6.2 allowed for remedies beyond damages, including injunctions, to protect Confidential Information. The changed Section 6.2 limits the client to damages only, essentially negating the original agreement's broader protection of 'Confidential Information', thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
